page: Microsoft Azure B2pls_v2 is a burstable virtual machine belonging to the Bpsv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an arm64 architecture utilizing an Ampere ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor with a clock speed of 3.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 2 physical cores and 2 vCPUs, with a single thread per core. It is equipped with 4.0 GB of memory, translating to 2.0 GB of RAM per core. Cache sizes are specified at 64 KB for L1d, 1.0 MB for L2, and 32 MB for L3. No local storage is bundled with this instance, though it is premium storage capable, and there are no integrated GPUs.

Performance benchmarks indicate that this server is positioned for low-intensity workloads. While single-core stress-ng and single-threaded gzip compression achieve average ratings, multi-core and memory-bound operations generally fall into the weak or poor categories. For instance, the Geekbench single-core score is 1111.0 and the multi-core score is 2010.0. Memory bandwidth is limited, with large block read-only speeds at 27.02 GB/sec and write speeds at 16.61 GB/sec. Complex tasks such as ray tracing, image processing, and LLM inference on small models show poor performance metrics.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the B2pls_v2 offers a budget-friendly entry point for resource-constrained applications due to its burstable CPU allocation model and low memory density. It is designed for workloads with variable CPU utilization that do not require sustained high performance. Suitable use cases include lightweight web serving, basic database operations, development or testing environments, and simple background tasks that can tolerate burstable CPU limits and lower memory bandwidth.
description: Microsoft Azure B2pls_v2 is an arm64 burstable virtual machine from the Bpsv2 family, featuring 2 vCPUs powered by an Ampere ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor at 3.0 GHz. It is configured with 4.0 GB of memory, providing 2.0 GB per core, and lacks local storage or integrated GPUs. Benchmark results indicate average single-core CPU performance but weak multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 2010.0. Given its burstable CPU allocation and low memory density, this instance represents a cost-efficient option for lightweight workloads. It is best suited for low-traffic web serving, basic database operations, and development environments that experience intermittent CPU demand.

tagline: A budget-friendly, burstable arm64 virtual machine with dual cores and low memory density for lightweight, variable workloads.

Features 2 vCPUs and 4.0 GB of system memory with no local storage
Utilizes a burstable CPU allocation model for variable workloads
